root_text.sn35.145:1.2: Yopi hetu, yopi paccayo rūpānaṁ uppādāya, sopi anattā.
root_text.sn35.145:1.3: Anattasambhūtā, bhikkhave, rūpā kuto attā bhavissanti.
root_text.sn35.145:1.9: Yopi hetu, yopi paccayo dhammānaṁ uppādāya, sopi anattā.
root_text.sn35.145:1.10: Anattasambhūtā, bhikkhave, dhammā kuto attā bhavissanti.
root_text.sn35.145:1.11: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpesupi nibbindati, saddesupi … gandhesupi … rasesupi … phoṭṭhabbesupi … dhammesupi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.145:1.12: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.145:1.13: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.145:1.2: The cause and reason that gives rise to sights is also not-self.
translation_text.sn35.145:1.3: Since sights are produced by what is not-self, how could they be self?
translation_text.sn35.145:1.9: The cause and reason that gives rise to ideas is also not-self.
translation_text.sn35.145:1.10: Since ideas are produced by what is not-self, how could they be self?
translation_text.sn35.145:1.12: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.145:1.13: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
